questions.question: What is the 72-hour emergency medication supply policy?
questions.final_message: {"mode": "FACTUAL", "direct_answer": "Florida state law requires participating pharmacies to provide a 72-hour (three-day) supply of certain medications to members awaiting prior authorization, ensuring continuity of therapy.", "sections": []}

questions.question: How can a provider determine whether a service requires prior authorization?
questions.final_message: {"mode": "FACTUAL", "direct_answer": "Providers can determine if a service requires prior authorization by using the Pre-Auth Check Tool (the primary method for checking by service code) or consulting the Authorization Requirements Policy document. For member-specific questions, contact Member Services at 1-866-796-0530 or behavioral health at 1-844-477-8313.", "sections": []}

questions.question: What are the weekend and after-hours on-call phone numbers?
questions.final_message: {"mode": "FACTUAL", "direct_answer": "For weekend and after-hours on-call support, contact Provider Services at 1-844-477-8313 for authorization requests, or Member Services at 1-866-796-0530 (TTY: 1-800-955-8770) for general questions.", "sections": []}

questions.question: Summarize the Secure Provider Portal registration process.
questions.final_message: {"mode": "FACTUAL", "direct_answer": "To register for the Secure Provider Portal, providers must request access by registering for a username and password. The available documentation provides limited detail beyond this basic registration requirement.", "sections": []}

questions.question: What is the process for claims reconsiderations and disputes?
questions.final_message: {"mode": "FACTUAL", "direct_answer": "To request a claim reconsideration or dispute, include the original claim number and relevant supporting documentation with your complaint submission. Reconsiderations from claim editing follow the standard provider claims process.", "sections": []}
